guardrail: static projection of the public register; no facts added here

data.method: Reshapes the existing permitted_activity_yield cells (one per jurisdiction) into the cross-jurisdiction view the flagship §4.5 describes. Asserts NO new facts: every field is copied from a record. The functional boundary and its per-jurisdiction placement are read from the records' binding_status / evidence_tier / source.
data.finding: Independent legislative systems are converging on the same functional boundary around stablecoin yield: yield paid for merely HOLDING the instrument is prohibited, while ACTIVITY-LINKED rewards are permitted. The boundary is decided by the same functional test across jurisdictions (conversion-direction, ownership-disclosure, timing-independence, yield-attribution, audit-trail — the Architecture paper's test).
data.discipline: Convergence is asserted at citable depth ONLY where the cell is tier1_legal + in_force_enacted + resolution_text AND both sides of the functional line are documented — which today is the United States alone. Singapore is recorded as a sibling restriction. In-force one-sided holder-yield prohibitions (EU, HK, JP, AE) are recorded on the prohibited side but the two-sided line is carried as backlog for them, not asserted as drawing the same line. Switzerland is the documented counter-example. Drafts are flagged as would-align-if-enacted. This is the register's own citable-purity discipline; it is what keeps the convergence a finding, not an impression.

anchor.instrument: GENIUS Act §4(a)(11); OCC NPRM §15.10(c)(4) / anti-evasion §15.10(c)(6); FDIC NPRM §350.3(b)(4); CLARITY Act (H.R. 3633) §404(c)(1) (Tillis-Alsobrooks text, 1 May 2026; affirmed final 5 May 2026)
anchor.pinpoint: GENIUS Act Sec. 4(a)(11) (no interest/yield in connection with holding, use, or retention)

line_both_sides.holder_yield: prohibited — GENIUS Act §4(a)(11): no interest/yield 'in connection with the holding, use, or retention' of a payment stablecoin (in force).
line_both_sides.activity_rewards: permitted — the pending market-structure layer (Senate Banking substitute, Tillis–Alsobrooks compromise) allows stablecoin rewards / activity-linked incentives while prohibiting yield for holding a balance (carried as a flagged pending overlay).

sibling.instrument: MAS Single-Currency Stablecoin framework (Aug 2023) — issuer activity restrictions
sibling.pinpoint: SCS framework: no lending/staking by SCS issuers; SGD/G10 label scope
sibling.sibling_form: The MAS Single-Currency Stablecoin framework forbids the issuer from lending or staking the reserve, holding the token to a pure means of payment rather than a yield product — a sibling expression of the same principle. binding_status=finalized_policy_pending, so not asserted at citable depth.

counter_example.pinpoint: Yield permitted; bank guarantee must cover interest (structural constraint, not prohibition)
counter_example.counter_form: Paying yield to holders is NOT prohibited — the clearest survey example of a regime where holder yield is permitted but structurally constrained (FINMA Guidance 06/2024; the bank guarantee must cover interest). Recorded explicitly so the convergence is not overstated.

holder_prohibition_in_force.pinpoint: Art. 50 (no interest paid to EMT holders)
holder_prohibition_in_force.two_sided_line_status: backlog — the holder-yield prohibition is in force and citable-quality, but the activity-rewards-permitted half is not yet verified for this jurisdiction, so the full two-sided convergence line is not asserted here.

holder_prohibition_in_force.pinpoint: Sch. 2 s.15 (no interest on issued stablecoins)
holder_prohibition_in_force.two_sided_line_status: backlog — the holder-yield prohibition is in force and citable-quality, but the activity-rewards-permitted half is not yet verified for this jurisdiction, so the full two-sided convergence line is not asserted here.

holder_prohibition_in_force.instrument: Payment Services Act (Electronic Payment Instruments regime)
holder_prohibition_in_force.pinpoint: Yield — holders not remunerated; issuer earns reserve interest
holder_prohibition_in_force.two_sided_line_status: backlog — the holder-yield prohibition is in force and citable-quality, but the activity-rewards-permitted half is not yet verified for this jurisdiction, so the full two-sided convergence line is not asserted here.

holder_prohibition_in_force.pinpoint: Yield — prohibited onshore; ADGM permits reserve income but bans investment/savings promotion
holder_prohibition_in_force.two_sided_line_status: backlog — the holder-yield prohibition is in force and citable-quality, but the activity-rewards-permitted half is not yet verified for this jurisdiction, so the full two-sided convergence line is not asserted here.

draft_would_align.instrument: Draft Digital Asset Basic Act (Value-Stabilised Assets Act track)
draft_would_align.pinpoint: Yield (draft) — prohibition on interest/discounts to holders; NOT in force
draft_would_align.status_note: The draft prohibits interest/returns to holders; would align this jurisdiction with the prohibited side if enacted (see the contingent event in the event calendar).

draft_would_align.pinpoint: Yield (draft) — prohibition on interest/other returns; NOT in force
draft_would_align.status_note: The draft prohibits interest/returns to holders; would align this jurisdiction with the prohibited side if enacted (see the contingent event in the event calendar).

backlog_or_not_applicable.instrument: SI 2026/102 (silent on yield); Bank of England Nov 2025 materials (60/40 backing; pass-through constrained)
backlog_or_not_applicable.pinpoint: BoE Nov 2025 backing-split / pass-through position; FCA rules pending
backlog_or_not_applicable.status_note: Backlog: yield is not expressly addressed in SI 2026/102; the BoE Nov-2025 pass-through position and FCA rules are pending (pending_proposal).

backlog_or_not_applicable.instrument: Lei 14.478/2022 (Marco Legal dos Ativos Virtuais); BCB implementing rulemaking
backlog_or_not_applicable.pinpoint: Res BCB 520/2025 (VASPs may not offer credit to clients or raise funds from the public except via share issuance; client virtual assets may not be used for proprietary operations except staking and qualified/professional-investor transactions)
backlog_or_not_applicable.status_note: Backlog: the VASP framework and BCB implementing rulemaking are still being finalized (firm_summary); pass-through treatment not yet at resolution_text depth.

backlog_or_not_applicable.instrument: PBOC et al. Notice on Preventing Token Fundraising Risks (2017); related circulars
backlog_or_not_applicable.pinpoint: Prohibition context — no permitted issuer to which a yield rule could attach
backlog_or_not_applicable.status_note: Not applicable: private issuance is prohibited, so there is no lawful domestic issuer to which a yield rule could attach; the yield question does not arise.

provenance.note: Every field is copied from an existing permitted_activity_yield record; this product only reshapes and tiers them.
provenance.generalizes_to: The same shape applies to monetary_sovereignty and other typed dimensions; convergence is checkable because yield is one of the typed dimensions every jurisdiction record carries.
